body_txt: The main distinction between this and the Acting for Two tropes is that almost all of the Acting for Two tropes operate on the assumption that the two (or more) characters look identical. Here, the point is merely that the two (or more) roles are intended to be played by the same person for some effect. So, in a way, this trope is a subtrope of Acting for Two, and a supertrope of some of the tropes listed on that page. Compare Identical Grandson , Identical Stranger . Often used to hint towards a Two Aliases, One Character situation when both are played by the same actor. Contrast Cast as a Mask , where two different actors are cast to conceal the fact that they're playing the same character.
